questions.q: What is the primary role of microbes in sewage treatment?
questions.answer: The primary role of microbes in sewage treatment is to break down organic matter, reducing BOD and COD, and producing a harmless effluent.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: Microbes are used in the production of _______________ (e.g., insulin, vaccines) through recombinant DNA technology.
Explain the role of Rhizobium in legume plants. (4 marks)
questions.answer: Rhizobium is a symbiotic nitrogen-fixing bacterium that lives in the root nodules of legume plants. It converts atmospheric nitrogen into a form that can be used by the plant, thereby reducing the need for nitrogen-based fertilizers. This mutualistic relationship benefits both the plant and the bacterium.

questions.q: Which of the following is NOT a benefit of using microbes in food production? (1 mark)
What is the purpose of using microbes in bioactive molecules production? (2 marks)
questions.answer: The purpose of using microbes in bioactive molecules production is to produce antibiotics, enzymes, and other bioactive compounds that have various applications in medicine, agriculture, and industry.

questions.q: State the importance of microorganisms in the production of antibiotics. (3 marks)
questions.answer: Microorganisms are the primary source of antibiotics, which are used to treat bacterial infections. Many antibiotics are produced through fermentation, and microbes such as Streptomyces and Penicillium are commonly used for this purpose.

questions.q: True or False: All microbes are pathogenic. (1 mark)
Explain the role of decomposer microbes in the ecosystem. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Decomposer microbes, such as bacteria and fungi, play a crucial role in the ecosystem by breaking down dead organic matter and recycling nutrients. They release nutrients back into the soil, making them available to plants, and help maintain soil fertility and structure.

questions.q: Which microbe is commonly used for the production of citric acid? (1 mark)
What is the significance of using microbes in bioremediation? (3 marks)
questions.answer: Microbes are used in bioremediation to clean up pollutants and toxic substances from the environment. They can break down or transform pollutants into harmless substances, making the environment safer and reducing the risk of pollution.
